---
title: "16S analyses"
author: "Isabella, Rebecca"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: github_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

## set working directory
setwd("../Agri_Contaminants")

## load required packages
library("devtools") ## this needs to be installed and loaded prior to running
### the following:
# install_github("helixcn/seqRFLP")
## Bioconductor packages
# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#    install.packages("BiocManager")
#BiocManager::install("phyloseq")
#BiocManager::install("DESeq2")

# install_github("JCSzamosi/aftersl1p@*release")

## load packages:
library("tidyverse") ## ggplot, dplyr, et c.
library("readxl")
library("knitr")
library("cowplot")
library("ggbeeswarm") ## prevent overlapping data points in ggplot
library("ggrepel") ## ensure text doesn't overlap in plots
library("car")
library("emmeans")
library("FSA") ## dunn's style test
library("phyloseq")
library("vegan")
library("pheatmap")
library("caret") ## findCorrelation func


## not sure these are needed
#library("Polychrome") ## generate randomized discrete colour palettes
#library("paletteer") ## generate colour palettes
#library("cluster")
#library("data.table")
#library("grid")
#library("ape")
#library("phangorn")

#library("seqinr") 
#library("phytools")
library("seqRFLP") ## dataframe2fas function
#library("DESeq2") 
#library("circlize")

# library("patchwork") ## couldn't load

# library("AfterSl1p") ## couldn't install

# library("ShortRead") ## couldn't install
```

## Import and format data

### OTU table, taxonomy assignment

```{r load_data}
##### load OTU file (rownames as sequences, cols as samples) #####
otufile <- "./raw_data/seqtab_nochim_transposed_RD-Mar2025_v4.csv"
otu_df <- read.csv(otufile, row.names = 1)

## extract ASV sequences
seqs <- rownames(otu_df)
## save asv sequences
seqs_df <- as.data.frame(seqs)
rownames(seqs_df) <- paste0("OTU", 1:nrow(seqs_df))
write.csv(seqs_df, file="./16S_analyses_files/ASV_sequencesv4R1.csv")
seqs.fasta <- dataframe2fas(seqs, file="./16S_analyses_files/ASVseqs.fasta")
## get rid of ASVs as rownames
otu.rn <- otu_df
rownames(otu.rn) <- NULL
## save otu file
save(otu.rn, file = "./16S_analyses_files/otu_cleaned.Rda")

## initial plotting (distribution of reads)
long_otu <- otu.rn %>% 
  gather(key = "Study_ID", value = "n_seqs")

sampling_coverage <- long_otu %>% 
  group_by(Study_ID) %>% 
  summarise(n_seqs = sum(n_seqs))
## negative controls, water samples

ggplot(sampling_coverage, 
       aes(x=n_seqs)) + 
       geom_density() # density plot

ggplot(sampling_coverage,
       aes(x=n_seqs)) + 
    geom_histogram(binwidth=5000) + 
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,170000)) 
# to get count, coord_cartesian() zooms in on parts of histogram, 
# can change bin width

ggplot(sampling_coverage,
       aes(x=1, y=n_seqs)) + 
geom_jitter() + 
scale_y_log10() +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 12000), linetype = 2)
# position on x axis is randomized so points don't overlap eachother, 
# y is the actual number of sequences, putting on log scale to see if there 
# is a threshold of points that some seqs fall beneath, where is the critical 
# mass of seqs

sampling_coverage %>% 
  arrange(n_seqs) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=1:nrow(.), y=n_seqs)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,50), ylim=c(0, 25000)) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 12000), linetype = 2)
# arranging samples in order of n_seqs, see number of samples we have on the 
# x axis and number of seqs in each of those samples, where the shoulder is, 
# that's probably where we want to cut, also zooming into shoulder
save(sampling_coverage, file = "./16S_analyses_files/sampling_coverage.Rdata")

##### taxa file (rownames as ASVs, cols as taxonomic information) #####
taxfile <- "./raw_data/taxa_RD-Mar2025_v4_silva1382WSP.csv"
tax_df <- read.csv(taxfile, row.names = 1)
all(seqs == rownames(tax_df)) ## TRUE
## get rid of rownames
tax.rn <- tax_df
rownames(tax.rn) <- NULL
## save taxonomy file
save(tax.rn, file = "./16S_analyses_files/tax_cleaned.Rda")
```

### Metadata file

Include phenotype information from direct and indirect experiments

```{r metadata_format}
## load sample info
sample_info <- 
  read_csv(
    "./raw_data/Sample info sheet - updated21Sept2025.csv")

## get rid of unnecessary cols
sample_info.s <- sample_info %>%
  select("ID", "Sample_ID", "Study_ID",
         "Sampling_Date",
         "Sample_Type","Sample_Treatment",
         "species","pot_ID", "Spiking_level",
         "Amendment")

## load sampling coverage info
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/sampling_coverage.Rdata") ## loads sampling_coverage

meta_full <- left_join(sample_info.s, sampling_coverage, by = "Study_ID")

## Add in timepoints
meta_full.sum <- meta_full %>%
  group_by(Sample_ID) %>%
  summarize(paste(`Sampling_Date`, collapse = " | "),
            paste(`Study_ID`, collapse = " | "))

## create timepoints sampling
meta_full$timepoint <- ifelse(is.na(meta_full$Sampling_Date) == TRUE,
                              "Source",
                        ifelse(meta_full$Sampling_Date == "2024-07-11",
                               "TP1",
                           ifelse(meta_full$Sampling_Date %in% 
                                    c("2024-08-06", ## radish
                                      "2024-08-18", ## lettuce
                                      "2024-08-19", ## pea (first batch)
                                      "2024-08-26", ## pea (second batch)
                                      "2024-09-01"), ## GH: Soil controls
                                  "TP2",
                                  "Source")
                           ))

## look at n_seqs depending on sample type
ggplot(meta_full, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = n_seqs)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  coord_flip()

## look at n_seqs depending on sample treatment
ggplot(meta_full, aes(x = Sample_Treatment, y = n_seqs)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = timepoint)) +
  coord_flip()

## exclude water samples + autoclaved soils (source)
meta <- meta_full %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(Sample_Treatment, "water") &
         !str_detect(Sample_Treatment, "Autoclaved")) %>%
  droplevels(.)
## 138 samples to include going forward

## plot distribution after excluding samples
ggplot(meta,
       aes(x=n_seqs)) + 
       geom_density() # density plot

meta %>% 
  arrange(n_seqs) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=1:nrow(.), y=n_seqs)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(10000, 100000)) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 12000), linetype = 2)

## add in other formatting for metadata file (factor specifications)
### Recode levels
treat_ordered <- c("control-0", ## reference
                   "Garden Soil",
                   "Biosolids",
                   "GH: Soil only",
                   "GH: Soil + BS",
                   "RW-0",
                   "RW-1",
                   "RW-2",
                   "BS-0",
                   "BS-1",
                   "BS-2")

meta$treat.rn <- factor(meta$Sample_Treatment, 
                                levels = treat_ordered)

meta$species <- ifelse(is.na(meta$species) == FALSE,
                            paste0(meta$species),
                            "no_plant")
# unique(meta$species)
# [1] "no_plant" "P"        "R"        "L" 
meta$species <- factor(meta$species,
                            levels = c("no_plant", "L", "R", "P"))

meta$species_tp <- ifelse(is.na(meta$species) == FALSE,
                            paste0(meta$species,"_",meta$timepoint),
                            "no_plant")
# unique(meta$species_tp)
# [1] "no_plant" "P_TP2"    "R_TP1"    "L_TP1"    "R_TP2"    "L_TP2"    "P_TP1" 
meta$species_tp <- factor(meta$species_tp,
                            levels = c("no_plant_Source", "no_plant_TP2",
                                       "L_TP1", "R_TP1","P_TP1",
                                       "L_TP2", "R_TP2", "P_TP2"))

meta$tp_treat <- paste(meta$timepoint, meta$treat.rn, sep = "_")
meta$tp_treat <- as.factor(meta$tp_treat)
meta$species_treat <- paste(meta$species, meta$treat.rn, sep = "_")
meta$species_treat <- as.factor(meta$species_treat)

### relevel Amendment to compare to control
meta$Amendment <- factor(meta$Amendment,
                                levels = c("control","RW","BS"))
### make new factor (spikeFac, ordered)
meta$spikeFac <- factor(meta$Spiking_level,
                               levels = c("SL-0","SL-1","SL-2"),
                               ordered = TRUE)
### make new factor (spikeFac, ordered)
meta$timepoint <- factor(meta$timepoint,
                               levels = c("Source","TP1","TP2"),
                               ordered = TRUE)

## add in trait data from direct/indirect experiments
## save combined data frame
load(file = "./direct_indirect-combined.Rda") ## loads direct.w

## combine into one
meta <- left_join(meta, direct.w, by = "pot_ID")

save(meta, file = "./16S_analyses_files/metadata_cleaned.Rda")
write.csv(meta, "./16S_analyses_files/metadata_cleaned.csv", row.names = FALSE)

### subset otu file to just the samples of interest (in experiment)
samples_to_include <- unique(meta$Study_ID)

## load otu file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/otu_cleaned.Rda") ## loads otu.rn
otu.f <- otu.rn %>%
  select(all_of(samples_to_include))

all(meta$Study_ID == colnames(otu.f)) ## TRUE

### Overview of Seq run (colSums adds up number of reads for each sample)
sort(colSums(otu.f)) ## num of reads for each sample (we had 138 total) 
sort(colSums(otu.f !=0)) ## this is sorting the num of ASVs for ea. sample

save(otu.f, file = "./16S_analyses_files/OTU-tab_cleaned.Rda")
```

## Create phyloseq objects

Making phyloseq object
This link explains what these variables mean in phyloseq:
https://joey711.github.io/phyloseq/import-data.html

```{r phyloseq}
## load relevant files
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/tax_cleaned.Rda") ## loads tax.rn
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/metadata_cleaned.Rda") ## loads meta
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/OTU-tab_cleaned.Rda") ## loads otu.f

## ensuring sample and OTU names match
rownames(otu.f) <- paste0("OTU", 1:nrow(otu.f))
rownames(tax.rn) <- paste0("OTU", 1:nrow(tax.rn))
rownames(meta) <- meta$Study_ID

## create the phyloseq object
OTU <- otu_table(otu.f, taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
TAX <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax.rn))
SAMPLE <- sample_data(meta)
### need to reset rownames so they match
rownames(SAMPLE) <- meta$Study_ID
### put them together
ps <- phyloseq(OTU, TAX, SAMPLE)

samplesums <- sort(sample_sums(ps))
write.csv(samplesums, file="./16S_analyses_files/readcounts_per_sample_sorted.csv")
plot(samplesums) ## distribution of sampling depth

save(ps, file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq.Rda") ## saves full phyloseq

## Filter table of bacterial taxa

### host DNA?
(tax.rn.host <- tax.rn %>%
  filter(Family == "Mitochondria") %>%
  summarize(count = n()))
## 390 OTUs

(tax.rn.rhizo <- tax.rn %>%
  filter(Family == "Rhizobiaceae") %>%
  summarize(count = n()))
## 75 OTUs

## subset to only bacteria, excluding mitochondria
dat_lessHOST <- subset_taxa(ps, Kingdom=="Bacteria", 
                            Family!="Mitochondria")
### save
save(dat_lessHOST, file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST.Rda")
write.csv(t(otu_table(dat_lessHOST)), 
          file="./16S_analyses_files/otu_table-noHOST.csv")

# glom to phylum level, this function merges taxa based on their taxonomic 
# rank, grouping into broader categories, was originally phylum, doesn't have 
# to be this data aggregation step, Example, Imagine a phyloseq object with 
# thousands of OTUs. Using tax_glom at the Genus level would merge all OTUs 
# belonging to the same genus, resulting in a much smaller set of genus-level 
# taxa.

## glom to family level
# rare_ps_family <- tax_glom(rare_ps, taxrank="Family")
# write.csv(otu_table(rare_ps_family), 
#           file="./16S_outputs/otu_table_noHOST_rarified_family.csv")
# save(rare_ps_family, 
#      file = "./16S_outputs/phyloseq_noHOST_rarified_family.Rda")
### on non-rarified (in case it matters)
# noHost_family <- tax_glom(dat_lessHOST, taxrank="Family")
# write.csv(otu_table(noHost_family), 
#           file="./16S_outputs/otu_table_noHOST_family.csv")
# save(noHost_family, 
#      file = "./16S_outputs/phyloseq_noHOST_family.Rda")
# ### after removing rare ASVs (in case it matters)
# rel_abund_family <- tax_glom(rel_abun_all_prune, taxrank="Family")
# write.csv(otu_table(rel_abund_family), 
#           file="./16S_outputs/otu_table_noHOST_prune1_family.csv")
# save(rel_abund_family, 
#      file = "./16S_outputs/phyloseq_noHOST_prune1_family.Rda")
```

## Rarefaction curve

Using the vegan package: 
https://fromthebottomoftheheap.net/2015/04/16/drawing-rarefaction-curves-with-custom-colours/

```{r rarify_data}
## load relevant file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/OTU-tab_cleaned.Rda") ## loads otu.f
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/metadata_cleaned.Rda") ## loads meta

## transpose OTU (samples as rows, ASVs as cols)
otu.t <- t(otu.f)
raremax <- min(rowSums(otu.t)) ## rowsums now adds up reads
raremax ## 12162

## sort cols by rowSums, pick bottom 25
row_sums <- rowSums(otu.t)
sorted_indices_asc <- order(row_sums)
otu.s <- otu.t[sorted_indices_asc, ]
otu.25 <- otu.s[1:25, ]

## testing the code: set up plotting parameters
col <- c("black", "darkred", "forestgreen", 
         "orange", "blue", "yellow", "hotpink")
lty <- c("solid", "dashed", "longdash", "dotdash")
pars <- expand.grid(col = col, lty = lty, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
head(pars)

## creating the initial plot
out <- with(pars[1:25, ],
            rarecurve(otu.25, step = 20, sample = raremax, col = col,
                      lty = lty, label = FALSE))

## pull out required parameters from out
Nmax <- sapply(out, function(x) max(attr(x, "Subsample")))
Smax <- sapply(out, max)

## colour curves by treatment
rownames(meta) <- meta$Study_ID

## sort meta to match otu.50
meta_ordered <- meta[rownames(otu.25), ]

## set colours to grouping var (treat)
### determine how many treatment groups
length(levels(meta_ordered$treat.rn)) ## 11
mycols <- c("gray", ## unamended controls  
            rep("black",4),   ## source controls
            "lightblue",      ## RW-0
            "cornflowerblue", ## RW-2
            "blue",           ## RW-3
            "#EABD8C",        ## BS-0
            "#FFAD00",        ## BS-1
            "#B06500")        ## BS-2

# Set levels of the factor to match the order of colours in mycols
grp <- factor(meta_ordered$treat.rn, 
              levels = levels(meta_ordered$treat.rn))  
# or use unique(meta_ordered$treat.rn) if needed
cols <- mycols[as.numeric(grp)]

png(filename = "./16S_analyses_files/figs/rarefaction_curve.png",
    width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
plot(c(1, max(Nmax)), c(1, max(Smax)), xlab = "Sample Size",
     ylab = "ASVs", type = "n")
abline(v = raremax)
for (i in seq_along(out)) {
    N <- attr(out[[i]], "Subsample")
    lines(N, out[[i]], col = cols[i], lwd = 2)
}
dev.off()

include_graphics("./16S_analyses_files/figs/rarefaction_curve.png")
```

## Alpha diversity

Run on rarified data in which host DNA is removed

Rarefaction is generally recommended for alpha diversity metrics 
(e.g., Shannon, Simpson, Observed OTUs) because these are sensitive to 
sampling depth. It ensures that differences in diversity are due to biological 
variation, not sequencing effort.

Shannon
What it measures:
* Richness (number of taxa)
* Evenness (how evenly distributed the taxa are)
Interpretation:
* Higher values = more diverse communities.
* Sensitive to rare taxa — even low-abundance species contribute to the index.
* Typical range: Usually between 1.5 and 3.5 for ecological datasets. Higher
  for soil

InvSimpson
What it measures:
* Dominance and evenness.
* Less sensitive to rare taxa; emphasizes common taxa.
Interpretation:
* Higher values = more even communities.
* A community dominated by one or few taxa will have a low inverse Simpson 
  value.
* Typical range: Starts at 1 (minimum diversity); higher values indicate 
  more diversity.


### Calculate diveristy (shannon, simpson)

Need to normalize to garden soil to get diversity loss

To capture how diversity changes between T1 and T2, 
we calculated rate of recovery relative to the initial loss:

RR = (Div T2 - Div T1) / Div initial (garden soil)

expressing the change between TP2 and TP1 
as a fraction of the initial diversity:
* Positive value → diversity increased from TP1 to TP2 (recovery).
* Negative value → diversity decreased further from TP1 to TP2 (continued loss).
* Magnitude → how large the change is relative to the initial diversity baseline.

```{r alpha_div-calc}
## load relevant phyloseqs
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST.Rda") ## loads dat_lessHOST
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/metadata_cleaned.Rda") ## loads meta

# rarefy, rarefying to 12000 based on curve
min_lib <- min(sample_sums(dat_lessHOST))
rare_ps <- rarefy_even_depth(dat_lessHOST,
                           sample.size= min_lib,
                           verbose=FALSE,
                           replace = FALSE)
## save
save(rare_ps, file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST_rare.Rda")
write.csv(t(otu_table(rare_ps)), 
          file="./16S_analyses_files/otu_table-noHOST_rare.csv")

## calculate richness metrics to pair with metadata
richness.df <- estimate_richness(rare_ps, 
                                 measures = 
                                   c("Shannon",
                                     "InvSimpson"))

## correction to InvSimp (x100)
richness.df$InvSimpson.c <- richness.df$InvSimpson /100

## create a col for sample ID
richness.df$Study_ID <- rownames(richness.df)

## Combine with sample metadata
meta <- left_join(meta, richness.df, by = "Study_ID")

## recovery rates
### convert to long for traits
meta.l <- meta %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c"),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "units"
  )
### 138 samples x 2 traits = 276 rows

### calculate loss of diversity (relative to Garden Soil)
meta.GS <- meta.l %>%
  filter(Sample_Treatment == "Garden Soil") %>%
  group_by(metric) %>%
  summarize(mean_GS = mean(units, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup(.)

GS_shannon <- meta.GS$mean_GS[meta.GS$metric == "Shannon"]
GS_simpson <- meta.GS$mean_GS[meta.GS$metric == "InvSimpson.c"]

## add in control means to long df
meta.l <- meta.l %>%
  left_join(
    meta.GS,
    by = c("metric")
  ) %>%
  mutate(div.loss = log2(units/mean_GS))

## convert back to wide (each row = sample)
meta.w <- meta.l %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = metric,
    values_from = div.loss,
    names_prefix = "div.loss.",
    id_cols = Study_ID
  )
## 138 samples

### add back to meta
meta <- left_join(meta, meta.w, by = c("Study_ID"))

## save formatted file
save(meta, file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda")

## pivot_wider for timepoint
meta.tp <- meta %>%
  filter(Sample_Type == "Experimental soil") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = timepoint,
    values_from = c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c",
                    "div.loss.Shannon",
                    "div.loss.InvSimpson.c"),
    id_cols = c("pot_ID","species","Amendment",
                "spikeFac","treat.rn")
  ) %>%
  ## calculate diversity recovery rates (RR)
  mutate(div.RR.Shannon = 
    (Shannon_TP2 - Shannon_TP1) / GS_shannon,
         div.RR.Simpson = 
    (InvSimpson.c_TP2 - InvSimpson.c_TP1) / GS_simpson
         )

## add back in trait data
load(file = "./direct_indirect-combined.Rda") ## loads direct.w

## combine into one
meta.tp <- left_join(meta.tp, direct.w, by = "pot_ID")

## save formatted file
save(meta.tp, file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.w.Rda")
```

### Plots: alpha diversity

```{r plot_alpha}
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

##### Plots (species, timepoint, treatment) #####


metric.list <- c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c",
                 "div.loss.Shannon","div.loss.InvSimpson.c")
### function
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha.func.R")

plots.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_alpha_div, 
                df = meta %>%
                  filter(!treat.rn %in% 
                           c("GH: Soil only",
                             "GH: Soil + BS",
                             "Biosolids")) %>%
                          droplevels(.),
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      simplify = FALSE)

### Combine plots
plots <- plot_grid(plots.out[["Shannon"]] +
                         labs(y = "Shannon"),
                       plots.out[["InvSimpson.c"]] +
                         labs(y = "Inv. Simpson (/100)"),
                       ncol = 1,
                       nrow = 2,
                       align = "v",
                       rel_heights = c(1,1))
plots
### save plots
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-alpha_div.png",
       width=12, height=6, units = "in")
include_graphics("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-alpha_div.png")
saveRDS(plots, file = "./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-alpha_div.rds")

## recovery rates

## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.w.Rda") ## loads meta.tp

##### Plots (species, treatment) #####

### function
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha.RR.func.R")

metric.list <- c("div.RR.Shannon","div.RR.Simpson")

plots.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_alpha_div, 
                df = meta.tp,
                USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                simplify = FALSE)

### Combine plots
plots <- plot_grid(plots.out[["div.RR.Shannon"]] +
                         labs(y = "Shannon \n RR"),
                       plots.out[["div.RR.Simpson"]] +
                         labs(y = "Inv. Simpson \n RR") +
                     theme(
                       strip.text.x = element_blank()),
                       ncol = 1,
                       nrow = 2,
                       align = "v",
                       rel_heights = c(1,1))
plots
### save plots
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-alpha_div.RR.png",
       width=8, height=6, units = "in")
include_graphics("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-alpha_div.RR.png")
```

### Models

#### M1a: species x amend (for ea. timepoint separately)

```{r m1a-species.vs.amend}
## M1: species x amendment (for ea. timepoint separately)
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

## set contrasts
options(contrasts=c("contr.sum","contr.poly")) 

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/models/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

## 1)  species x amendment (for each timepoint sep)
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha_species.vs.amend.func.R")

## create list of traits/amends to loop through:
metric.list <- c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       times = time.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, sep = "_")
  )

## run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div_species.vs.amend, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta %>%
                  filter(!species %in% c("no_plant") &
                           Spiking_level %in% c("SL-0"))))

## save models
aov <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

### format
aov$pval <- signif(aov$`Pr(>F)`, 3)
aov$sig <- ifelse(aov$pval< 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
aov$stat <- signif(aov$`F value`, 3)

## pivot wider for each model term
aov.w <- aov %>%
  select(-c(`F value`,"Pr(>F)","pval", "Sum Sq")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = term,
    values_from = c(stat, Df, sig)
  ) ## 4 traits total, 2 timepoints, 8 rows

## formatting
aov.f <- aov.w %>%
  mutate(Amendment = paste0("F [", Df_Amendment, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_Amendment,
                                sig_Amendment),
         Species = paste0("F [", Df_species, ", ",
                           Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_species,
                           sig_species),
         `Species x Amendment` = 
                             paste0("F [", 
                               `Df_species:Amendment`, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ",
                               `stat_species:Amendment`,
                               `sig_species:Amendment`)
         ) %>%
  select(metric, time, Species, Amendment, `Species x Amendment`)

### CONTRASTS
cont <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 3) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

## round pval
cont$pval <- signif(cont$p.value, 3)
### format
cont$sig <- ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
cont$stat <- signif(cont$t.ratio, 3)

cont$stat.f <- paste0("t = ", cont$stat, ", ", 
                    "df = ", cont$df,
                    cont$sig)
## estimates
cont$log2FC <- log2(cont$ratio)
cont$LCL <-signif(log2(cont$lower.CL), 3)
cont$UCL <- signif(log2(cont$upper.CL), 3)
cont$log2FC_CL <- paste0(signif(cont$log2FC, 3), " [",
                        cont$LCL," to ",
                        cont$UCL,"]")
## save as R data file
save(cont, file = paste0(output_dir, "cont1a-div_species.vs.amend.Rdata"))

## save relevant output for table
cont.s <- cont %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast_info = paste0(species, "_", contrast, ": ", 
                           stat.f, "; ", 
                           "log2FC [95% CL] = ", log2FC_CL)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, time, contrast_info) %>%
  group_by(metric, time) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_contrasts = paste(contrast_info, collapse = " | ")
    )

## add to anova
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, cont.s, by = c("metric","time")) 
## save
write.csv(aov.f, file = paste0(output_dir, 
                               "aov1a-div_species.vs.amend.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
kable(aov.f)

## emmeans
emm <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 2) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(emm, file = paste0(output_dir, 
                               "emm1a-div_species.vs.amend.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

```

#### M1b: species x amend (diversity recovery)

```{r m1b-species.vs.amend_RR}
## M1b: species x amendment
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.w.Rda") ## loads meta.tp

## set contrasts
options(contrasts=c("contr.sum","contr.poly")) 

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/models/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

## 1)  species x amendment (across timepoints)
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha.RR_species.vs.amend.func.R")

## create list of traits/amends to loop through:
metric.list <- c("div.RR.Shannon","div.RR.Simpson")

## run function
div.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                   div.RR_species.vs.amend,
                   df = meta.tp %>%
                     filter(spikeFac == "SL-0") %>%
                     droplevels(.),
                   simplify = FALSE, 
                   USE.NAMES = TRUE)

## save models
aov <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

### format
aov$pval <- signif(aov$`Pr(>F)`, 3)
aov$sig <- ifelse(aov$pval< 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
aov$stat <- signif(aov$`F value`, 3)

## pivot wider for each model term
aov.w <- aov %>%
  select(-c(`F value`,"Pr(>F)","pval", "Sum Sq")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = term,
    values_from = c(stat, Df, sig)
  ) ## 2 metrics total, 1 timepoint, 2 rows

## formatting
aov.f <- aov.w %>%
  mutate(Amendment = paste0("F [", Df_Amendment, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_Amendment,
                                sig_Amendment),
         Species = paste0("F [", Df_species, ", ",
                           Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_species,
                           sig_species),
         `Species x Amendment` = 
                             paste0("F [", 
                               `Df_species:Amendment`, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ",
                               `stat_species:Amendment`,
                               `sig_species:Amendment`)
         ) %>%
  select(metric, Species, Amendment, `Species x Amendment`)

### CONTRASTS
cont <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 3) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

## round pval
cont$pval <- signif(cont$p.value, 3)
### format
cont$sig <- ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
cont$stat <- signif(cont$t.ratio, 3)

cont$stat.f <- paste0("t = ", cont$stat, ", ", 
                    "df = ", cont$df,
                    cont$sig)
## estimates
cont$log2FC <- log2(cont$ratio)
cont$LCL <-signif(log2(cont$lower.CL), 3)
cont$UCL <- signif(log2(cont$upper.CL), 3)
cont$log2FC_CL <- paste0(signif(cont$log2FC, 3), " [",
                        cont$LCL," to ",
                        cont$UCL,"]")
## save as R data file
save(cont, file = paste0(output_dir, "cont1b-div.RR_species.vs.amend.Rdata"))

## save relevant output for table
cont.s <- cont %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast_info = paste0(species, "_", contrast, ": ", 
                           stat.f, "; ", 
                           "log2FC [95% CL] = ", log2FC_CL)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, contrast_info) %>%
  group_by(metric) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_contrasts = paste(contrast_info, collapse = " | ")
    )

## add to anova
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, cont.s, by = c("metric")) 
## save
write.csv(aov.f, paste0(output_dir, "aov1b-div.RR_species.vs.amend.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
kable(aov.f)

## emmeans
emm <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 2) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(emm, paste0(output_dir, "emm1b-div.RR_species.vs.amend.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

```

#### M2a: species x contaminant (for ea. amendement + TP separately) 

```{r m2a-species.vs.contam}
## M2: species x contam (for ea. timepoint and amend separately)
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

## set contrasts
options(contrasts=c("contr.sum","contr.poly")) 

## var lists
amend.list <- c("RW","BS")
metric.list <- c("Shannon",
                 "InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       times = time.list,
                       amends = amend.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, amends, sep = "_")
  )

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/models/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

source("./Source_code/mod-alpha_species.vs.contam.func.R")

### run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div_species.vs.contam, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      amends = comb_df$amends, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta %>%
                  filter(!species %in% c("no_plant") &
                           !Amendment %in% c("control"))))

## save models
aov <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
### format
aov$pval <- signif(aov$`Pr(>F)`, 3)
aov$sig <- ifelse(aov$pval< 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
aov$stat <- signif(aov$`F value`, 3)

## pivot wider for each model term
aov.w <- aov %>%
  select(-c(`F value`,"Pr(>F)","pval", "Sum Sq")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = term,
    values_from = c(stat, Df, sig)
  ) ## 4 traits total, 2 timepoints, 2 amendments = 16 rows

## formatting
aov.f <- aov.w %>%
  mutate(`Spiking level (SL)` = paste0("F [", Df_spikeFac, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_spikeFac,
                                sig_spikeFac),
         Species = paste0("F [", Df_species, ", ",
                           Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_species,
                           sig_species),
         `Species x SL` = paste0("F [", 
                               `Df_species:spikeFac`, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ",
                               `stat_species:spikeFac`,
                               `sig_species:spikeFac`)
         ) %>%
  select(metric, Amendment = amend, time, Species, 
         `Spiking level (SL)`, `Species x SL`)

### CONTRASTS
cont <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 3) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

## round pval
cont$pval <- signif(cont$p.value, 3)
### format
cont$sig <- ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
cont$stat <- signif(cont$t.ratio, 3)

cont$stat.f <- paste0("t = ", cont$stat, ", ", 
                    "df = ", cont$df,
                    cont$sig)
## estimates
cont$Est_r <- signif(cont$estimate, 3)
cont$LCL <- ifelse(is.na(cont$lower.CL) == TRUE,
                   signif(cont$asymp.LCL, 3),
                   signif(cont$lower.CL, 3))
cont$UCL <- ifelse(is.na(cont$upper.CL) == TRUE,
                   signif(cont$asymp.UCL, 3),
                   signif(cont$upper.CL, 3))
cont$Est_CL <- paste0(cont$Est_r, " [",
                        cont$LCL," to ",
                        cont$UCL,"]")
## save as R data file
save(cont, file = paste0(output_dir, "cont2a-div_species.vs.contam.Rdata"))

## save relevant output for table
cont.s <- cont %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast_info = paste0(species, "_", contrast, ": ", 
                           stat.f, "; ", 
                           "Est. [95% CL] = ", Est_CL)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, time, Amendment = amend, contrast_info) %>%
  group_by(metric, time, Amendment) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_contrasts = paste(contrast_info, collapse = " | ")
    )

## species comps
### CONTRASTS
cont.pw <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 4) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

## round pval
cont.pw$pval <- signif(cont.pw$p.value, 3)
### format
cont.pw$sig <- ifelse(cont.pw$p.value < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(cont.pw$p.value < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", cont.pw$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(cont.pw$p.value < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", cont.pw$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(cont.pw$p.value < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", cont.pw$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
cont.pw$stat <- signif(cont.pw$t.ratio, 3)

cont.pw$stat.f <- paste0("t = ", cont.pw$stat, ", ", 
                    "df = ", cont.pw$df,
                    cont.pw$sig)
## estimates
cont.pw$log2FC <- signif(log2(cont.pw$ratio), 3)
cont.pw$LCL <- signif(log2(cont.pw$lower.CL), 3)
cont.pw$UCL <- signif(log2(cont.pw$upper.CL), 3)
cont.pw$log2FC_CL <- paste0(cont.pw$log2FC, " [",
                        cont.pw$LCL," to ",
                        cont.pw$UCL,"]")

## save relevant output for table
cont.pw.s <- cont.pw %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast_info = paste0(contrast, ": ",
                           stat.f, "; ", 
                           "log2FC [95% CL] = ", log2FC_CL)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, time, Amendment = amend, contrast_info) %>%
  group_by(metric, time, Amendment) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_conts.species = paste(contrast_info, collapse = " | ")
    )

## add to anova
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, cont.s, by = c("metric","time","Amendment")) 
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, cont.pw.s, by = c("metric","time","Amendment")) 
## save
write.csv(aov.f, paste0(output_dir, "aov2-alpha_contam.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

## emmeans
emm <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 2) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(emm, paste0(output_dir, "emm2a-div_species.vs.contam.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

```

#### M2b: species x contaminant (diversity recovery) 

```{r m2b-species.vs.contam_RR}
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.w.Rda") ## loads meta.tp

## set contrasts
options(contrasts=c("contr.sum","contr.poly")) 

## var lists
amend.list <- c("RW","BS")
metric.list <- c("div.RR.Shannon",
                 "div.RR.Simpson")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       amends = amend.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, amends, sep = "_")
  )

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/models/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

source("./Source_code/mod-alpha.RR_species.vs.contam.func.R")

### run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div.RR_species.vs.contam, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      amends = comb_df$amends, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta.tp %>%
                                        filter(
                                          !Amendment %in% c("control")
                                        ))
                  )

## save models
aov <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
### format
aov$pval <- signif(aov$`Pr(>F)`, 3)
aov$sig <- ifelse(aov$pval< 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
aov$stat <- signif(aov$`F value`, 3)

## pivot wider for each model term
aov.w <- aov %>%
  select(-c(`F value`,"Pr(>F)","pval", "Sum Sq")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = term,
    values_from = c(stat, Df, sig)
  ) ## 4 traits total, 2 timepoints, 2 amendments = 16 rows

## formatting
aov.f <- aov.w %>%
  mutate(`Spiking level (SL)` = paste0("F [", Df_spikeFac, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_spikeFac,
                                sig_spikeFac),
         Species = paste0("F [", Df_species, ", ",
                           Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_species,
                           sig_species),
         `Species x SL` = paste0("F [", 
                               `Df_species:spikeFac`, ", ",
                                Df_Residuals, "] = ",
                               `stat_species:spikeFac`,
                               `sig_species:spikeFac`)
         ) %>%
  select(metric, Amendment = amend, Species, 
         `Spiking level (SL)`, `Species x SL`)

### CONTRASTS
cont <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 3) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

## round pval
cont$pval <- signif(cont$p.value, 3)
### format
cont$sig <- ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(cont$p.value < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", cont$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
cont$stat <- signif(cont$t.ratio, 3)

cont$stat.f <- paste0("t = ", cont$stat, ", ", 
                    "df = ", cont$df,
                    cont$sig)
## estimates
cont$Est_r <- signif(cont$estimate, 3)
cont$LCL <- ifelse(is.na(cont$lower.CL) == TRUE,
                   signif(cont$asymp.LCL, 3),
                   signif(cont$lower.CL, 3))
cont$UCL <- ifelse(is.na(cont$upper.CL) == TRUE,
                   signif(cont$asymp.UCL, 3),
                   signif(cont$upper.CL, 3))
cont$Est_CL <- paste0(cont$Est_r, " [",
                        cont$LCL," to ",
                        cont$UCL,"]")
## save as R data file
save(cont, file = paste0(output_dir, "cont2b-div.RR_species.vs.contam.Rdata"))

## save relevant output for table
cont.s <- cont %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast_info = paste0(species, "_", contrast, ": ", 
                           stat.f, "; ", 
                           "Est. [95% CL] = ", Est_CL)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, Amendment = amend, contrast_info) %>%
  group_by(metric, Amendment) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_contrasts = paste(contrast_info, collapse = " | ")
    )

## add to anova
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, cont.s, by = c("metric","Amendment")) 
## save
write.csv(aov.f, paste0(output_dir, "aov2b-div.RR_species.vs.contam.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
kable(aov.f)

## emmeans
emm <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 2) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(emm, paste0(output_dir, "emm2b-div.RR_species.vs.contam.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

```

#### Post-hoc tests (supp fig)

```{r alpha_div_PHT}
## figures (contrasts)
load(file = 
       "./16S_analyses_files/models/cont1a-div_species.vs.amend.Rdata") ## loads cont

## formatting
cont$amend <- ifelse(grepl("BS", cont$contrast, fixed = FALSE),
                      "BS", "RW")
cont$amend <- factor(cont$amend,
                      levels = c("RW",
                                 "BS"))
cont$species <- factor(cont$species,
                       levels = c("L",
                                  "R",
                                  "P"))
cont$time <- factor(cont$time,
                       levels = c("TP1","TP2"))

## run function for each metric
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha_amend_PHT.func.R")

## metrics
metric.list <- c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c")

## run
plot.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_div_species.vs.amend, 
                df = cont,
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      simplify = FALSE)
## combine
plots_amend <- plot_grid(
  plot.out[["Shannon"]] + 
    labs(x = "Shannon"),
  plot.out[["InvSimpson.c"]] +
    labs(x = "Inv. Simpson",
         y = expression(log[2]~"Fold Change (95% CL)")),
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = 2,
  align = "v",
  labels = c("A", "C"))
plots_amend

## M2

## emms (emmeans)
emms <- 
  read_csv("./16S_analyses_files/models/emm2a-div_species.vs.contam.csv")

## linear contrasts (to get sig linear trends)
load(file = 
       "./16S_analyses_files/models/cont2a-div_species.vs.contam.Rdata") ## loads cont

## include both quadratic & linear terms
cont.w <- cont %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = contrast,
    values_from = c("Est_CL", "pval"),
    id_cols =  c("species","metric","time","amend")
  )

## combine with emms
emms <- emms %>%
  left_join(
    cont.w,
    by = c("species","amend","time","metric")
  )

## formatting
emms$amend <- factor(emms$amend,
                      levels = c("RW",
                                 "BS"))

emms$species <- factor(emms$species,
                       levels = c("L",
                                  "R",
                                  "P"))
emms$spikeFac <- factor(emms$spikeFac, 
  levels = c(
  "SL-0","SL-1","SL-2"))

## run function for each metric
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha_contam_PHT.func.R")

## metrics
metric.list <- c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c")

## run
plot.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_div_species.vs.contam, 
                df = emms,
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      simplify = FALSE)
## combine
plots_contam <- plot_grid(
  plot.out[["Shannon"]] + 
    labs(y = "EM mean (95% CL)"),
  plot.out[["InvSimpson.c"]] +
    labs(y = "EM mean (95% CL)",
         x = "Contaminant spiking level"),
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = 2,
  align = "v",
  labels = c("B", "D"))
plots_contam

## combine them together
plots <- plot_grid(plots_amend, plots_contam,
                   ncol = 2,
                   nrow = 1,
                   align = "h",
                   labels = NULL)
plots

ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-div_PHT.png", 
       width = 10, height = 6, 
       units = "in")
```

#### Post-hoc tests (supp fig - div recovery)

```{r alpha_div.RR_PHT}
## figures (contrasts)
load(file = 
       "./16S_analyses_files/models/cont1b-div.RR_species.vs.amend.Rdata") ## loads cont

## formatting
cont$amend <- ifelse(grepl("BS", cont$contrast, fixed = FALSE),
                      "BS", "RW")
cont$amend <- factor(cont$amend,
                      levels = c("RW",
                                 "BS"))
cont$species <- factor(cont$species,
                       levels = c("L",
                                  "R",
                                  "P"))

## run function for each metric
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha.RR_amend_PHT.func.R")

## metrics
metric.list <- c("div.RR.Shannon","div.RR.Simpson")

## run
plot.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_div.RR_species.vs.amend, 
                df = cont,
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      simplify = FALSE)
## combine
plots_amend <- plot_grid(
  plot.out[["div.RR.Shannon"]] + 
    labs(x = "Shannon \n RR"),
  plot.out[["div.RR.Simpson"]] +
    labs(x = "Inv. Simpson \n RR",
         y = expression(log[2]~"Fold Change (95% CL)")),
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = 2,
  align = "v",
  labels = c("A", "C"))
plots_amend

## M2

## emms (emmeans)
emms <- 
  read_csv("./16S_analyses_files/models/emm2b-div.RR_species.vs.contam.csv")

## linear contrasts (to get sig linear trends)
load(file = 
       "./16S_analyses_files/models/cont2b-div.RR_species.vs.contam.Rdata") ## loads cont

## include both quadratic & linear terms
cont.w <- cont %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = contrast,
    values_from = c("Est_CL", "pval"),
    id_cols =  c("species","metric","amend")
  )

## combine w/ emmeans
emms <- emms %>%
  left_join(
    cont.w,
    by = c("species","amend","metric")
  )

## formatting
emms$amend <- factor(emms$amend,
                      levels = c("RW",
                                 "BS"))

emms$species <- factor(emms$species,
                       levels = c("L",
                                  "R",
                                  "P"))
emms$spikeFac <- factor(emms$spikeFac, 
  levels = c(
  "SL-0","SL-1","SL-2"))

## run function for each metric
source("./Source_code/plot-alpha.RR_contam_PHT.func.R")

## metrics
metric.list <- c("div.RR.Shannon","div.RR.Simpson")

## run
plot.out <- sapply(metric.list, 
                plot_div.RR_species.vs.contam, 
                df = emms,
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      simplify = FALSE)
## combine
plots_contam <- plot_grid(
  plot.out[["div.RR.Shannon"]] + 
    labs(y = "EM mean (95% CL)"),
  plot.out[["div.RR.Simpson"]] +
    labs(y = "EM mean (95% CL)",
         x = "Contaminant level"),
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = 2,
  align = "v",
  labels = c("B", "D"))
plots_contam

## combine them together
plots <- plot_grid(plots_amend, plots_contam,
                   ncol = 2,
                   nrow = 1,
                   align = "h",
                   labels = NULL)
plots

ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-div.RR_PHT.png", 
       width = 10, height = 6, 
       units = "in")
```

#### Kruskal Wallis tests

Non-parametric test, with fewer assumptions
* Does not assume normality
* Compares ranked values across groups
* Robust to outliers and non-homogenous variances

```{r Kruskal-Wallis}
## load file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

## impact of species (control-0 only)

## source
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha_species_KW.func.R")

## var lists
metric.list <- c("Shannon",
                 "InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       times = time.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, sep = "_")
  )

### run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div_species_KW, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta %>%
                  filter(!species %in% c("no_plant") &
                           treat.rn %in% c("control-0"))))

## combine dfs
KW_species_df <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(KW_species_df, "./16S_analyses_files/models/mod-div_species_KW.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

## impact of amendment (SL-0 only) within ea species

## source
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha_amend_KW.func.R")

## var lists
metric.list <- c("Shannon",
                 "InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
crop.list <- c("L", "R", "P")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       times = time.list,
                       crops = crop.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, crops, sep = "_")
  )

### run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div_amend_KW, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      crops = comb_df$crops, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta %>%
                  filter(!species %in% c("no_plant") &
                           Spiking_level %in% c("SL-0"))))

## combine dfs
KW_amend_df <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(KW_amend_df, "./16S_analyses_files/models/mod-div_amend_KW.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

## impact of contaminant concentration (within amends, exclude control)

## source
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha_contam_KW.func.R")

## var lists
amend.list <- c("RW","BS")
metric.list <- c("Shannon",
                 "InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
crops.list <- c("L", "R", "P")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list,
                       times = time.list,
                       amends = amend.list,
                       crops = crops.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, crops, amends, sep = "_")
  )

### run function
div.out <- mapply(FUN = div_contam_KW, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      crops = comb_df$crops,
                      amends = comb_df$amends, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta %>%
                  filter(!species %in% c("no_plant") &
                           !Amendment %in% c("control"))))

## combine dfs
KW_contam_df <- lapply(div.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)
write.csv(KW_contam_df, "./16S_analyses_files/models/mod-div_contam_KW.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

#### M3: Alpha diversity vs plant traits (direct + indirect)

Does microbiome diversity correlate with: 
* Plant traits -- direct exposure experiment
* Microbiome impacts on plant traits -- microbiome inoculation experiment


* Shannon diversity being crop-dependent suggests that plant identity is shaping 
microbial richness/evenness early and consistently.
* The Inverse Simpson × timepoint interaction implies that dominance patterns are 
shifting over time, potentially due to stress, competition, or microbial turnover.

```{r alpha_vs_trait.corr}
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

## traits to iterate through
trait.list <- c("direct.dry_shoot_weight",
                "direct.shoot_root_ratio",
                "direct.per_plant_weight",
                "direct.shoot_moisture",
                "direct.germination_perc",
                "direct.survival_perc.corr",
                ## pea-only traits
                "direct.dry_pod_weight.corr",
                "direct.infected_peas_perc.corr",
                "direct.NDVI_mean",
                "direct.pods",
                "direct.flowers")
            
## create long df for traits 
meta.l <- meta %>%
  filter(species != "no_plant") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(trait.list),
    names_to = "trait",
    values_to = "units"
  ) ## 11 traits by 126 samples = 1386 rows


## create list of traits/amends to loop through:
metric.list <- c("Shannon","InvSimpson.c")
time.list <- c("TP1", "TP2")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list, 
                       traits = trait.list,
                       times = time.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, traits, times, sep = "_")
  ) 

## ANOVAs to test for interactions (species x diversity)

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/models/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

## source function
source("./Source_code/mod-alpha.vs.trait_aov.func.R")

lm.out <- mapply(FUN = mod_alpha.vs.trait_aov, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      traits = comb_df$traits, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      times = comb_df$times,
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = meta.l)
                   )

## combine dfs
### ANOVAs
aov <- lapply(lm.out, `[[`, 1) %>%
  bind_rows(.)

### format
aov$pval <- signif(aov$`Pr(>F)`, 3)
aov$sig <- ifelse(aov$pval< 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.01, 
                   paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.05, 
                   paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(aov$pval < 0.1, 
                   paste0(", p = ", aov$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
aov$stat <- signif(aov$`F value`, 3)

aov <- aov %>%
  mutate(term = str_replace_all(term,
                                "log\\(metric_value\\)",
                                   "diversity"))
## pivot wider for each model term
aov.w <- aov %>%
  select(-c(`F value`,"Pr(>F)","pval", "Sum Sq")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = term,
    values_from = c(stat, Df, sig)
  ) ## 8 traits x 2 metrics x 2 times = 32 rows

## formatting
aov.f <- aov.w %>%
  mutate(
   Species = paste0("F [", Df_species, ", ",
                     Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_species,
                     sig_species),
   Diversity =  paste0("F [", Df_diversity, ", ",
                          Df_Residuals, "] = ", stat_diversity,
                          sig_diversity),
   `Diversity x Species` =  
     paste0("F [", 
            `Df_diversity:species`, ", ",
                          Df_Residuals, "] = ", `stat_diversity:species`,
                          `sig_diversity:species`)
         ) %>%
  select(metric, trait, time, Diversity, Species,
         `Diversity x Species`) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = metric,
    values_from = c("Diversity", "Species", 
                    "Diversity x Species")
  )

##### correlations #####

load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.w.Rda") ## loads meta.tp

source("./Source_code/mod-alpha.vs.trait_corr.func.R")

# Identify relevant columns
metric.list <-  c("Shannon_TP1","InvSimpson.c_TP1",
                  "Shannon_TP2","InvSimpson.c_TP2")
trait.list <- c("direct.survival_perc.corr",
                "direct.shoot_root_ratio",
                "direct.per_plant_weight",
                "direct.dry_pod_weight.corr")
species.list <- c("L", "R", "P")

## run function
results <- run_correlations(data = meta.tp, 
                            metric.list, 
                            trait.list,
                            species.list)

# Combine results
results_df <- bind_rows(results)

## split cols in results_df
results_df <- results_df %>%
  separate(Diversity, into = c("metric", 
                               "timepoint"), sep = "_")

## round pval
results_df$pval <- signif(results_df$P_value_correlation, 3)
### format
results_df$sig <- ifelse(results_df$P_value_correlation < 0.001, ", p < 0.001",
            ifelse(results_df$P_value_correlation < 0.01, paste0(", p = ", results_df$pval, "**"),
            ifelse(results_df$P_value_correlation < 0.05, paste0(", p = ", results_df$pval, "*"),
            ifelse(results_df$P_value_correlation < 0.1, paste0(", p = ", results_df$pval, "."),
                               ", p > 0.1"))))
## test stats
results_df$R2 <- signif(results_df$R_squared, 3)
## test stats
results_df$r <- signif(results_df$Pearson_corr, 3)


results_df$stat <- paste0("R2=", results_df$R2, ", ", 
                    "r = ", results_df$r,
                    results_df$sig)

## save relevant output for table
res.sig <- results_df %>%
  filter(P_value_correlation < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_corrs = paste0(Species, "_", metric,
                           ": ", stat)
    ) %>%
  select(metric, time = timepoint, trait = Trait, sig_corrs) %>%
  group_by(trait, time) %>%
  summarize(
    sig_corrs = paste(sig_corrs, collapse = " | ")
    )

## add to anova
aov.f <- left_join(aov.f, res.sig, by = c("trait","time")) 

## save
write.csv(aov.f, paste0(output_dir,
                             "aov3-alpha.vs.trait.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# visualize Pearson correlations as a heatmap
heatmap_data <- results_df %>%
  mutate(Diversity = paste0(metric, "_", timepoint)) %>%
  select(Diversity, Trait, Species, Pearson_corr) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c("Trait", "Species"),
              values_from = Pearson_corr)

heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(heatmap_data[,-1])
rownames(heatmap_matrix) <- heatmap_data$Diversity

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
output_dir <- "./16S_analyses_files/figs/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# Save the heatmap
p <- pheatmap(t(heatmap_matrix),
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
         breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
         main = "Pearson Correlation Heatmap",
         fontsize_row = 12,
         fontsize_col = 12,
         fontsize_number = 10)
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "./corr_plots/corr_heatmap.png"), 
       plot = p,
    width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 400)

## plots for pod weight (peas only)
meta.tp.l <- meta.tp %>%
  filter(species == "P") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(metric.list),
    names_to = "metric_tp",
    values_to = "value") %>%
   separate(metric_tp, 
            into = c("metric", "timepoint"), 
            sep = "_",
            remove = FALSE)
## 21 samples x 4 metrics = 84 rows

## set order  
meta.tp.l$metric <- factor(meta.tp.l$metric,
                          levels = c(
                            "Shannon",
                            "InvSimpson.c"
                          ))

metric_labs <- c(
  Shannon = "Shannon",
  InvSimpson.c = "Inv. Simpson (/100)"
)

# Plot
p <- ggplot(meta.tp.l, aes(x = value, 
                           y = direct.dry_pod_weight.corr)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, 
             aes(colour = treat.rn, shape = timepoint)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(linetype = timepoint), 
              method = "lm", colour = "black",
              se = FALSE, formula = y ~ x) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("gray", 
                                "lightblue", 
                                "cornflowerblue",
                                "blue", 
                                "#EABD8C", 
                                "#FFAD00", 
                                "#B06500")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(2, 17)) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(2, 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_x", ncol = 1,
             labeller = labeller(metric = metric_labs)) +
  guides(color = "none", shape = "none", linetype = "none") +
  labs(y = "Dry pod weight", x = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
print(p)

## save  
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "plot-div.vs.dryPodWeight.png"), 
       width = 8, height = 4, plot = p,
       units = "in")
saveRDS(p, file = paste0(output_dir,
                         "plot-div.vs.dryPodWeight.rds"))

```

## Beta diversity

PCoA relative abundance OTUs

If your samples have widely varying sequencing depths, rarefaction can help 
make comparisons more fair. If your depths are fairly uniform, you might skip 
rarefaction and use methods that account for library size (e.g., DESeq2, 
variance-stabilizing transformation, or CLR transformation for compositional 
data)

Use non-rarefied data, but filter for rare taxa (< 0.1%)

```{r beta_PCoA}
## load relevant phyloseqs
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST.Rda") ## loads dat_lessHOST

## remove OTUs less than 0.1%
rel_abun_all <- transform_sample_counts(dat_lessHOST, function(x) x/sum(x))
rel_abun_all_prune <- 
  prune_taxa(taxa_sums(rel_abun_all) > 0.001, dat_lessHOST)
## check sampling depth
samplesums <- sort(sample_sums(rel_abun_all_prune))
## save
save(rel_abun_all_prune, 
     file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST_pruned1.Rda")
write.csv(t(otu_table(rel_abun_all_prune)), 
          file="./16S_analyses_files/otu_table-noHOST_pruned1.csv")

## load metadata file
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/meta_div.Rda") ## loads meta

## PCoA plot with Bray Curtis distances
## ordinate
ord_data <- ordinate(dat_lessHOST, method = "PCoA", 
                            distance = "bray")

var_explained <- ord_data$values$Relative_eig * 100

# Extract ordination coordinates for samples
ord_df <- plot_ordination(dat_lessHOST, 
                          ord_data, 
                          axes = c(1, 2), justDF = TRUE)

# Check column names
head(ord_df)

## plot

## formatting
species_tp.names <- c(
    no_plant_Source = "Source",
    no_plant_TP2 = "Soil only: Harvest",
    L_TP1 = 'Lettuce: 1 wk PP',
    L_TP2 = 'Lettuce: Harvest',
    R_TP1 = 'Radish: 1 wk PP',
    R_TP2 = 'Radish: Harvest',
    P_TP1 = 'Pea: 1 wk PP',
    P_TP2 = 'Pea: Harvest'
  )

p <- ggplot(ord_df, aes(x = Axis.1, y = Axis.2, color = treat.rn)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = treat.rn, shape = species_tp),
             size = 4, stroke = 2,
             alpha = 0.7) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("gray",
                                "black",
                                "brown",
                                "green3",
                                "green4",
                                "lightblue",
                                "cornflowerblue",
                                "blue",
                                "#EABD8C",
                                "#FFAD00",
                                "#B06500")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(3, 4, 0, 1, 2, 15,16, 17),
                     labels = species_tp.names) +
  labs(x = paste0("PCoA Axis 1 [", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%]"),
       y = paste0("PCoA Axis 2 [", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%]"),
       shape = "Crop species: \n timepoint",
       colour = "Treatment") +
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2),
        shape = guide_legend(ncol = 2)) +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"), 
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=14), 
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=14), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12), 
        axis.text.y = element_text(size= 12), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10), 
        legend.title = element_text(size =10, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
p
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-PCoA_all_data.nofilt.png",
       width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", plot = p,
       dpi = 400)

#Extract Eigenvalues
eigenvalues <- ord_data$values$Eigenvalues

# Number of components
n <- length(eigenvalues)

# Calculate broken stick model
broken_stick <- sapply(1:n, function(i) {
  sum(1 / (i:n)) / n * 100
})

# Create a dataframe for plotting
scree_data <- data.frame(
  PrincipalCoordinate = 1:n,
  VarianceExplained = eigenvalues / sum(eigenvalues) * 100,
  BrokenStickModel = broken_stick
)

# Visualize observed vs. broken stick model (optional)
scree_bs <- ggplot(scree_data, 
                   aes(x = PrincipalCoordinate)) + 
  geom_line(aes(y = VarianceExplained),
                  color = "blue", 
                  linewidth = 1, 
                  linetype = "solid") + 
    geom_line(aes(y = BrokenStickModel), 
              color = "red", 
              linewidth = 1, 
              linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(aes(y = VarianceExplained)) +
    labs(title = "Scree Plot for PCoA and Broken Stick Model",
         x = "Principal Coordinate",
         y = "Variance Explained (%)") + 
    theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"))
scree_bs
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plot-scree_all_data.nofilt.png",
       width = 6, height = 5, units = "in", plot = scree_bs,
       dpi = 400)

## function to compare treatments within each timepoint
source("./Source_code/plot-beta.func.R")

time.list <- c("TP1","TP2")
metric.list <- c("PCoA","NMDS")
comb_df <- expand_grid(metrics = metric.list, times = time.list)
## Combine into a single string
comb_df <- comb_df %>%
  mutate(
    combs = paste(metrics, times, sep = "_")
  )
## run function
plots.out <- mapply(FUN = plot_beta, 
                      combs = comb_df$combs, 
                      times = comb_df$times, 
                      metrics = comb_df$metrics, 
                      USE.NAMES = TRUE, 
                      SIMPLIFY = FALSE,
                      MoreArgs = list(df = dat_lessHOST)
                   )

# Get shared limits

# Build the plot to access its components
p1_built <- ggplot_build(plots.out[["PCoA_TP1"]])
p2_built <- ggplot_build(plots.out[["PCoA_TP2"]])

# Extract y-axis limits
(y1_limits <- range(p1_built$layout$panel_scales_y[[1]]$range$range))
(y2_limits <- range(p2_built$layout$panel_scales_y[[1]]$range$range))

## combine plots (all crops)
fig1 <- plot_grid(plots.out[["PCoA_TP1"]] +
                    ggtitle("1 wk PP") +
                    coord_cartesian(ylim = y1_limits) +
                    theme(legend.position = "none"), 
                  plots.out[["PCoA_TP2"]] +
                    ggtitle("Harvest") +
                    coord_cartesian(ylim = y1_limits) +
                    theme(legend.position = "none"),
                  rel_widths = c(1, 1),
          ncol = 1,
          nrow = 2,
          align = "v",
          labels = NULL)
fig1

## save
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-PCoA.nofilt.png",
       width = 4, height = 8, units = "in", plot = fig1,
       dpi = 400)
saveRDS(fig1, file = "./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-PCoA.nofilt.rds")

## save legend
# Extract legend from one original plot (before theme(legend.position = "none"))
legend_plot <- plots.out[["PCoA_TP1"]]   # or any plot with legend
legend <- get_legend(legend_plot + 
                       theme(legend.position = "right"))
legend_scaled <- cowplot::ggdraw(legend) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0))

## save
saveRDS(legend_scaled, file = "./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-PCoA.legend.rds")

# NMDS: Get shared limits

# Build the plot to access its components
p1_built <- ggplot_build(plots.out[["NMDS_TP1"]])
p2_built <- ggplot_build(plots.out[["NMDS_TP2"]])

# Extract y-axis limits
(y1_limits <- range(p1_built$layout$panel_scales_y[[1]]$range$range))
(y2_limits <- range(p2_built$layout$panel_scales_y[[1]]$range$range))
(x1_limits <- range(p1_built$layout$panel_scales_x[[1]]$range$range))
(x2_limits <- range(p2_built$layout$panel_scales_x[[1]]$range$range))

fig2 <- plot_grid(plots.out[["NMDS_TP1"]] +
                    ggtitle("1 wk PP") +
                    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y2_limits[[1]],
                                             y1_limits[[2]]),
                                    xlim = c(x2_limits[[1]],
                                             x1_limits[[2]])) +
                    labs(x = "NMDS Axis 1",
                         y = "NMDS Axis 2") +
                    theme(legend.position = "none"),
                  plots.out[["NMDS_TP2"]] +
                    ggtitle("Harvest") +
                    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y2_limits[[1]],
                                             y1_limits[[2]]),
                                    xlim = c(x2_limits[[1]],
                                             x1_limits[[2]])) +
                     labs(x = "NMDS Axis 1",
                         y = "NMDS Axis 2"),
                  rel_widths = c(0.7, 1),
          ncol = 2,
          nrow = 1,
          align = "h",
          labels = NULL)
fig2
## save
ggsave("./16S_analyses_files/figs/plots-NMDS.nofilt.png",
       width = 10, height = 4, units = "in", plot = fig2,
       dpi = 400)

```

### Capscale analysis (dbRDA)

CAPSCALE (constrained analysis of principal coordinates) is a form of 
distance-based redundancy analysis (db-RDA), and it's particularly useful 
when you want to relate microbiome community structure (based on a distance 
matrix) to environmental variables.

```{r beta-capscale_dbRDA}
## load relevant data
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST_pruned1.Rda") 
## loads rel_abun_all_prune (ps object)

## load relevant data
load(file = "./16S_analyses_files/phyloseq-noHOST.Rda") 
## loads dat_lessHOST (ps object)


## subset to experimental samples
df.f <- 
  prune_samples(!sample_data(dat_lessHOST)$species %in%
                  "no_plant", dat_lessHOST)

## Extract OTU table and sample data
otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(phyloseq::otu_table(df.f))
  if (phyloseq::taxa_are_rows(df.f)) {
    otu_table_df <- t(otu_table_df)
  }
sample_data_df <- as(sample_data(df.f), "data.frame")

## examine correlations among traits and reduce (all species)

trait.list <- c(
                "wet_shoot_weight", 
                "dry_shoot_weight",
                "wet_root_weight", 
                "dry_root_weight",
                "dry_total_weight",
                "per_plant_weight",
                "shoot_moisture",
                "shoot_root_ratio",
                "germination_perc",
                "survival_perc.corr")

### select all traits
direct_cols_df <- direct[, trait.list]

## check correlations among traits
# Select numeric columns
numeric_cols <- direct_cols_df %>%
  select(where(is.numeric))

# Impute missing values with column means
imputed_df <- numeric_cols %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric),
                ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 
                         mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

# Correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(imputed_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Plot heatmap
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
       color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
       display_numbers = TRUE,
       cluster_rows = TRUE,
       cluster_cols = TRUE)

## remove highly correlated vars

# Find highly correlated variables
high_corr <- findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = 0.8)

# Remove them
reduced_traits <- numeric_cols[, -high_corr]

selected_traits.all <- names(reduced_traits)

# Correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(reduced_traits, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Plot heatmap
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
       color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
       display_numbers = TRUE,
       cluster_rows = TRUE,
       cluster_cols = TRUE)

## repeat for pea only traits

trait.list <- c(
                "wet_pod_weight.corr",
                "dry_pod_weight.corr",
                "per_pod_weight",
                "infected_peas_perc",
                "NDVI_mean",
                "pods",
                "flowers")

### select all traits
direct_cols_df <- direct[, trait.list]

## check correlations among traits
# Select numeric columns
numeric_cols <- direct_cols_df %>%
  select(where(is.numeric))

# Impute missing values with column means
imputed_df <- numeric_cols %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric),
                ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 
                         mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

# Correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(imputed_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Plot heatmap
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
       color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
       display_numbers = TRUE,
       cluster_rows = TRUE,
       cluster_cols = TRUE)

## remove highly correlated vars

# Find highly correlated variables
high_corr <- findCorrelation(cor_matrix, cutoff = 0.8)

# Remove them
reduced_traits <- numeric_cols[, -high_corr]

selected_traits.pea <- names(reduced_traits)

# Correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(reduced_traits, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Plot heatmap
pheatmap(cor_matrix,
       color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),
       display_numbers = TRUE,
       cluster_rows = TRUE,
       cluster_cols = TRUE)

## source function
source("./Source_code/mod-beta.vs.trait_dbRDA.func.R")

## run for each species individually, then for all
## iterate for each timepoint
time.list <- c("TP1","TP2")
species_list <- c("L", "P", "R")
results_list <- list()

for (sp in species_list) {
  df.sp <- prune_samples(sample_data(df.f)$species == sp, df.f)
  results_list[[sp]] <- sapply(time.list,
                               mod_beta.vs.trait_dbRDA,
                               df = df.sp,
                               include_species_term = FALSE,
                               simplify = FALSE, 
                               USE.NAMES = TRUE)
}

# For all species together
results_list[["all_species"]] <- 
  sapply(time.list, mod_beta.vs.trait_dbRDA, df = df.f,
         include_species_term = TRUE,
         simplify = FALSE, 
         USE.NAMES = TRUE)

## create figure panel

# Helper function to extract axis limits safely
get_axis_limits <- function(built_plots, axis = "x") {
  ranges <- lapply(built_plots, function(bp) {
    scale <- if (axis == "x") bp$layout$panel_scales_x[[1]] else bp$layout$panel_scales_y[[1]]
    scale$range$range
  })
  unlist(ranges)
}

# Collect plots
species <- c("L", "R", "P")
species_labels.TP1 <- c("Let: 1wkPP", 
                        "Rad: 1wkPP",
                        "Pea: 1wkPP")
species_labels.TP2 <- c("Let: Har", 
                        "Rad: Har",
                        "Pea: Har")

plots_tp1 <- lapply(species, function(s) results_list[[s]][["TP1"]][[1]])
plots_tp2 <- lapply(species, function(s) results_list[[s]][["TP2"]][[1]])

# Build plots to get axis limits
built_plots <- lapply(c(plots_tp1, plots_tp2), ggplot_build)

# Calculate limits with buffer
x_limits <- range(get_axis_limits(built_plots, "x"))
y_limits <- range(get_axis_limits(built_plots, "y"))

x_buffer <- 0.1 * diff(x_limits)
y_buffer <- 0.1 * diff(y_limits)

x_limits <- c(x_limits[1] - x_buffer, x_limits[2] + x_buffer)
y_limits <- c(y_limits[1] - y_buffer, y_limits[2] + y_buffer)

# Apply limits and formatting
plots_tp1 <- Map(function(p, lbl) {
  p + coord_cartesian(xlim = x_limits, 
                      ylim = y_limits) +
    ggtitle(lbl) +
    theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
          legend.position = "none")
}, plots_tp1, species_labels.TP1)

plots_tp2 <- Map(function(p, lbl) {
  p + coord_cartesian(xlim = x_limits, 
                      ylim = y_limits) +
    ggtitle(lbl) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}, plots_tp2, species_labels.TP2)

# Combine into figure
row_tp1 <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots_tp1, ncol = 3)
row_tp2 <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots_tp2, ncol = 3)

# Extract legend from one original plot (before theme(legend.position = "none"))
legend_plot <- results_list[["all_species"]][["TP1"]][[1]]  # or any plot with legend
legend <- get_legend(legend_plot + 
                       theme(legend.position = "right"))
legend_scaled <- cowplot::ggdraw(legend) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0))

## combine
fig <- plot_grid(
  plot_grid(row_tp1, 
            row_tp2, 
            nrow = 2),
  legend_scaled,
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(3, 0.4)  # reduce width for legend
)
fig

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
## set output directory
output_dir.fig <- "./16S_analyses_files/figs/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir.fig)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

## save
saveRDS(fig, file = 
          paste0(output_dir.fig, 
                 "plot-dbRDA.species.wtraits.nofilt.rds"))
ggsave(paste0(output_dir.fig, "plot-dbRDA.species.wtraits.nofilt.png"),
       width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", plot = fig,
       dpi = 400)

## save just pea
# Collect plots
species <- c("P")
species_labels.TP1 <- c("Pea: 1wkPP")
species_labels.TP2 <- c("Pea: Har")

plots_tp1 <- lapply(species, function(s) results_list[[s]][["TP1"]][[1]])
plots_tp2 <- lapply(species, function(s) results_list[[s]][["TP2"]][[1]])

# Build plots to get axis limits
built_plots <- lapply(c(plots_tp1, plots_tp2), ggplot_build)

# Calculate limits with buffer
x_limits <- range(get_axis_limits(built_plots, "x"))
y_limits <- range(get_axis_limits(built_plots, "y"))

x_buffer <- 0.1 * diff(x_limits)
y_buffer <- 0.1 * diff(y_limits)

x_limits <- c(x_limits[1] - x_buffer, x_limits[2] + x_buffer)
y_limits <- c(y_limits[1] - y_buffer, y_limits[2] + y_buffer)

# Apply limits and formatting
plots_tp1 <- Map(function(p, lbl) {
  p + coord_cartesian(xlim = x_limits, 
                      ylim = y_limits) +
    ggtitle(lbl) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}, plots_tp1, species_labels.TP1)

plots_tp2 <- Map(function(p, lbl) {
  p + coord_cartesian(xlim = x_limits, 
                      ylim = y_limits) +
    ggtitle(lbl) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}, plots_tp2, species_labels.TP2)

# # Combine into figure
# row_tp1 <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots_tp1, ncol = 3)
# row_tp2 <- plot_grid(plotlist = plots_tp2, ncol = 3)

# Extract legend from one original plot (before theme(legend.position = "none"))
legend_plot <- results_list[["all_species"]][["TP1"]][[1]]  # or any plot with legend
legend <- get_legend(legend_plot + 
                       theme(legend.position = "right"))
legend_scaled <- cowplot::ggdraw(legend) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0))

## combine
fig <- plot_grid(
  plot_grid(plots_tp1[[1]], 
            plots_tp2[[1]], 
            nrow = 2),
  legend_scaled,
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(3, 1)  # reduce width for legend
)
fig

## without legend
fig.wo.leg <- 
  plot_grid(plots_tp1[[1]], 
            plots_tp2[[1]], 
            nrow = 2,
            align = "v") # reduce width for legend
```
